audit_items.proof_trace: Both ldap_txn_end (L75) and ldap_txn_end_s (L108) call ber_alloc_t(LBER_USE_DER) then immediately pass the result to ber_printf without a null check; ber_alloc_t returns NULL on LBER_CALLOC failure (confirmed in liblber/io.c:269-272), and ber_printf asserts ber != NULL (encode.c:511) — crashing in debug or dereferencing NULL in NDEBUG release. passwd.c in the same library correctly checks ber == NULL and returns LDAP_NO_MEMORY (passwd.c:99-102), confirming this is an unintentional omission in txn.c.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker causes memory exhaustion on the LDAP client or proxy (e.g., by flooding large server responses), then triggers a transaction end operation; ber_alloc_t returns NULL, ber_printf dereferences it, crashing the process (DoS).
